title: Cyanobacteria Yolk-Shell Preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"39qbu","text":"Cyanobacteria as well as other photosynthetic microorganisms can be encapsulated within biohybrid nanomaterials. Cyanobacterial Yolk-shell are biohybrid nanostructures with a size of 1 to 6 µm, where the cell is encapsulated within a non-contacting silica nanocapsule. Yolk-shell encapsulation provides long-term cell viability, higher resistance against harsh environments and superior photosynthetic activiy.  In addition, engineering the colloidal packing allows tunable  shell-pore diameter for size-dependent permeability and introduction of  new functionalities for specific molecular recognition. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4uem9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"catl5","text":"Yolk-Shell structures can be easily synthetized via self-assebly of colloidal suspensions of silica nanoparticles as described in this protocol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6svan","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7g4pc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"7j1n0","text":"Fresh Cyanobacterial Culture","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"22fl3","text":"  ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"fq4g7","text":"Protamine Sulfate (Biochemistry Grade)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":38}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"95sse","text":"PBS x1 Buffer pH = 7.4","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2ql4o","text":"BG-11 Media (HEPES 10 mM pH = 8)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":32}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"15b91","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"87e8c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":953231,"guid":"436B3796341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"LUDOX® TMA colloidal silica","mol_weight":0,"sku":"420859","url":"https://www.sigmaaldrich.com/ES/en/product/aldrich/420859","cas_number":"7631-86-9","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":17,"name":"Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich)","link":"http://sigmaaldrich.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: 2800 rpm, °C, 10 minutes In sterility, centrifugue a suffcient ammount of exponentially growing cyanobacteria culture (20 to 100 mL) . OD 720 should be between 0.5 to 1. It is recommended to split the culture in two different sterile centrifugue tubes.

In sterility, discard the supernatant and add 1/10 th of the initial volume with sterile PBS buffer. Resuspend the cell pellet gently, gently flicking the tube or slowly pipetting up and down with a wide open pipette tip.

Take 1000 µL sample from the resuspended cells and measure the Optical Density at 720 nm using a Spectrophotometer. OD 720 should be 1.3 . If OD 720 is greater than 1.3 , go to step #2 and add more sterile PBS till reaching the required OD 720 value.

In Sterility, add 1/50 part (v/v) of the available resuspended cells volume of 5% protamine sulfate in sterile PBS. It is recommended to flick the tube up and down a couple of times and incubate the cells with protamine sulfate for 00:05:00 . (No agitation required)
2800 rpm, °C, 10 minutes Centrifugue the cells and discard the supernatant. IMPORTANT! Write the final volume of cell suspension in the tube before discarding the supernatant.
After centrifugation, resuspend the cell pellet in the same volume of cell suspension formerly used. Final OD 720 should still be around 1.3

Add the necessary ammount of Ludox(R) TMA colloidal suspension to reach a final concentration of 1 mg/mL in the cell suspension. V Ludox-TMA = VLudox (μL) = (VcellSuspension (mL)/418)* 1000 NOTE. Ludox TMA has a ρ = 1.23 g/mL and it's a colloidal suspension at 34% wt , then each mL of the stock will carry 418 mg of silica nanoparticles. To know the required ammount of Ludox, just divide your cell suspension volume by 418.
Incubate the suspension for 00:20:00 with mild agitation using a rocker shaker at minimum speed.

3600 rpm, °C, 4 minutes After incubation, centrifugue the former suspension and discard the supernatant . Wash the yolk-shell pellet with fresh BG-11 media using the same ammount of volume used during incubation (Other media could also be used for marine cyanobacteria strains).

Repeat the wash step go to step #9 , and resuspend the cells with fresh BG-11 media to a final OD 720 = 1. In this conditions yolk shell can be stored at room temperature and mild illumination for long periods of time.
creator: Jorge Fernández Méndez
